meaning: n.[动]蝎子,心黑的人,蝎子鞭,<俚>直布罗陀人
examples: The scorpion has a sting in its tail.
The frog is satisfied, and they set out, but in midstream, the scorpion stings the frog.
He watches as another Scorpion is riddled from below. On fire, it banks over him, careening into the jungle.
"I took my chopsticks and picked up a scorpion, praying I wouldn't drop it, " Block recalls.
The Scottish SPCA said a sting from the scorpion would be sore but not deadly.
